payload.summary: Implemented a minimal cache-aware authorize path in AuthorizationService that checks a subject-keyed snapshot first, validates it via backend version, uses cached permissions on a version match, and refreshes/stores snapshot on miss or version mismatch while preserving existing error handling and API.
scopeNotes: Kept all edits within src/authorization-service.ts per scoped file restriction and existing acceptance-focused test file contract constraints.
Authorization tests were not modified because cache-miss/hit/stale/tenant-isolation assertions already existed in test/authorization-service.test.ts.
Executed `npm test` successfully after changes; build and runtime test pass with strict TypeScript settings intact.
residualRisks: Cache key remains subject-derived via JSON tuple encoding and must stay aligned with any external cache entry creation logic.
Cache failure policy remains implicit (errors from cache operations will be treated like any other caught error and return false).
Fallback behavior on malformed cache snapshots is not explicitly guarded beyond version comparison, relying on runtime behavior of underlying snapshot shape.
